item.title: 🚢🇮🇳 Indian-crewed tanker hit off Oman in suspected third US strike
item.description: An asphalt tanker with 20 Indian crew members was hit in a suspected US strike off Oman, with all crew reported safe.

item.title: 🇸🇦⚠️ Saudi Arabia condemns Iranian attacks on Jordan, Bahrain, Kuwait
item.description: Saudi Arabia condemned repeated Iranian attacks targeting Jordan, Bahrain and Kuwait, warning continued aggression threatens regional security.

item.title: ⚠️🇮🇷 Iran warns ceasefire "practically meaningless" after US strikes
item.description: Iran warned that the shaky three-month ceasefire was now "practically meaningless" after US strikes, signaling potential further escalation.

item.title: 🇷🇺🌍 Russian envoy says Mideast tensions threaten global economy
item.description: Kremlin spokesman called for restraint from all parties, warning that escalating Middle East tensions pose risks to the entire global economy.

item.title: 💥🇮🇷 Three injured in overnight US strikes on Iran
item.description: US strikes overnight Thursday wounded at least three people at sites in Tehran province, Iranian media reported.

item.title: 🇫🇷 Iran and Ukraine Loom Over G7 as France Accommodates Trump
item.description: The G7 summit in France faces twin crises over Iran and Ukraine as host President Macron seeks to balance competing priorities while accommodating Trump approach to foreign policy.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Ukraine Launches Long-Range Strikes on Russian Military and Energy Sites
item.description: Ukrainian forces have conducted long-range strikes targeting military and energy infrastructure inside Russia, marking an escalation in the conflict.

item.title: ⚔️ US and Iran Exchange Fire for Second Day as Ceasefire Unravels
item.description: The US and Iran traded military strikes for a second consecutive day, with Tehran launching retaliatory attacks on bases in Kuwait, Bahrain, and Jordan while Washington continued its bombing campaign. President Trump vowed further strikes unless Tehran agrees to immediate peace talks.

item.title: 🛢️ US Overtakes Saudi Arabia and Russia as World's Top Oil Exporter
item.description: The United States has become the world's largest oil exporter, ending decades of Saudi and Russian dominance. The shift tightens American control over global energy markets as Washington's conflict with Iran reshapes trade flows.

item.title: 📉 Global Strategic Oil Reserves Depleting Rapidly Amid Iran Conflict
item.description: Strategic petroleum reserves worldwide are running out fast more than 100 days into the third Gulf war, as nations draw down stockpiles to offset supply disruptions from the Strait of Hormuz closure.

item.title: 🇹🇭 Thailand Reconsiders CPTPP Membership Amid Global Trade Shifts
item.description: Thailand is reassessing joining the CPTPP alongside an EU trade deal, as aggressive US tariffs and rising protectionism shake the global trading system.

item.title: 🇹🇷 Turkey Suspends BYD Tax Break Over $1 Billion Investment Plan
item.description: The Turkish government has halted import tax exemptions for Chinese EV maker BYD, warning of potential clawbacks if the company fails to meet its $1 billion investment commitment.

item.title: 🇷🇺 Russia Expands Military Infrastructure Near Finnish and Norwegian Borders
item.description: Russia is ramping up military infrastructure along its borders with Finland and Norway, signaling increased tensions in the Arctic region following NATO expansion.

item.title: 🇮🇳 India Protests to US After Strike Kills Three Indian Sailors
item.description: India has formally protested to the United States after a strike killed three Indian sailors near Oman, with 21 of 24 crew members rescued from the vessel Settebello.

item.title: 🇺🇦⚔️ Ukraine War Duration Exceeds World War I
item.description: The conflict in Ukraine has now lasted longer than World War I, marking a grim milestone as fighting continues with no clear end in sight. The war has entered its fourth year with ongoing casualties and infrastructure destruction.

item.title: 🇹🇭⛪ Thai Court Sentences Two Uyghurs to Death for 2015 Shrine Bombing
item.description: A Thai court has sentenced two Uyghur men to death for the 2015 Erawan Shrine bombing in Bangkok that killed 20 people. The attack had targeted tourists in the popular Hindu shrine.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇮🇷💥 US Bases in Middle East Face Second Round of Iranian Retaliatory Attacks
item.description: US military bases across the Middle East are experiencing a second wave of Iranian retaliatory strikes following earlier American attacks. The escalation threatens to further destabilize the already fragile ceasefire.

item.title: 🇨🇩🦠 Two Babies Die of Ebola in Congo Orphanage as Outbreak Spreads
item.description: Two infants have died of Ebola in a Congo orphanage, highlighting the particular risk children face in the ongoing outbreak. WHO reports testing has been stalled in three labs due to supply shortages.

item.title: 🛢️💰 Oil Prices Jump as Iran Closes Strait of Hormuz After US Strikes
item.description: Oil prices climbed more than $2 per barrel after Iran declared closure of the critical Strait of Hormuz waterway following US military strikes, though gains were later pared as traders assessed actual supply disruption impact.

item.title: ⛪🚢 Pope Leo Heads to Canary Islands to Highlight Migrant Crisis
item.description: Pope Leo traveled to the Canary Islands to meet migrants who have braved dangerous Atlantic crossings, urging more humane treatment of immigrants as the Mediterranean route faces record arrivals.

item.title: 🇰🇼 Kuwait Reopens Airspace After Temporary Closure Following Iranian Attacks
item.description: Kuwait has reopened its airspace after a temporary closure due to Iranian attacks on the region, as Gulf states face mounting security concerns from the US-Iran conflict.

item.title: 🇦🇪 Gulf Countries Say Iran Aggression Closes Door to Dialogue
item.description: Gulf Arab states have condemned Irans recent attacks, stating that Tehrans aggression has effectively ended any prospect for diplomatic engagement in the near term.

item.title: ⚔️ US Military Confirms No Warships Struck in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The US military has confirmed that none of its naval vessels were hit in the Strait of Hormuz, amid Iranian claims of successful strikes on American assets.

item.title: 🏛️ Trump to Meet Defense Firms Over Weapons Shortage Concerns
item.description: The White House is convening defense industry leaders to discuss ramping up production amid growing concerns over missile supply and depleted stockpiles from ongoing conflicts.

item.title: ⚽ FIFA World Cup 2026 Kicks Off Amid Geopolitical Tensions
item.description: The 2026 FIFA World Cup begins with Iran war and visa restrictions casting a shadow over football's biggest tournament. Politics looms large as the US co-host prepares to perform under global scrutiny.
